title: IT Manager
description: IT Manager Barbados Civil Aviation Authority Christ Church Not disclosed Permanent full-time Updated 11/02/2026 Apply Now Share Share this job Facebook Twitter Report This Job Save Save The IT Manager oversees and secures the Authority’s ICT systems to support regulatory excellence and operational efficiency, aligning technology and data assets with BCAA’s objectives while ensuring high-quality aeronautical information systems and compliance with international aviation standards. IT MANAGER - VACANCY NOTICE The Barbados Civil Aviation Authority (BCAA) is seeking a dynamic, strategic, and results-driven IT Manager to lead and safeguard the Authority’s Information and Communications Technology (ICT) environment. This is an exciting leadership opportunity for a forward-thinking ICT professional ready to drive innovation within a highly regulated and mission-critical environment. POSITION OVERVIEW This senior role is responsible for overseeing, managing, securing, and optimizing the Authority’s computer and ICT systems to support regulatory excellence and operational efficiency. The successful candidate will ensure that the BCAA’s technological and data assets are aligned with the Authority’s performance objectives, enabling the delivery of high-quality aeronautical information management systems and supporting Barbados’ compliance with international aviation standards. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES Lead the strategic, organizational, and operational planning of the Authority’s information systems. Manage, monitor, and secure the BCAA’s ICT and data assets, including cyber security, access controls, back-up procedures, operational continuity, and disaster recovery. Identify, recommend, and implement cost-effective ICT solutions including procurement, integration, upgrades, and system enhancements. Develop and manage the annual Information Systems budget. Oversee network infrastructure, servers, installations, migrations, and system performance to ensure reliability, stability, and availability. Supervise and guide IT personnel in network and systems administration. Ensure adherence to IT governance policies and regulatory compliance requirements. Provide expert technical advice and strategic recommendations to Senior Management. Foster innovation, continuous improvement, and effective user support across the Authority. Establish and maintain strategic partnerships with ICT service providers. Participate in policy development, service quality initiatives, and cross-functional projects. Represent the Authority at seminars, workshops, and professional forums. Perform other related duties as may be reasonably assigned. QUALIFICATIONS & EXPERIENCE A Master’s Degree or equivalent postgraduate professional designation in Computer Science, Information Technology, or related field, plus a minimum of three (3) years’ relevant experience in a similar leadership role; OR A Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent postgraduate professional designation in Computer Science, Information Technology, or related field, plus a minimum of five (5) years’ relevant experience in a similar leadership role. Candidates should also demonstrate: Expert knowledge of network administration. Advanced experience in data security and ICT systems management. Strong understanding of IT governance, compliance, and risk management. Working knowledge of the Barbados Civil Aviation Act and Regulations. Experience managing aeronautical information management services in accordance with ICAO Annex 15 and ISO 9001 standards (an asset). Experience with Geographic Information Systems (GIS) (an asset). Proven track record of high performance and leadership. Ability to manage multiple projects under pressure and deliver results within prescribed timelines. Strong analytical, troubleshooting, and systems-thinking capabilities. CORE COMPETENCIES & SKILLS: Exceptional customer and systems user support skills. Strategic thinking and innovation. Strong organisational, planning, budgeting, and project management skills. Critical thinking and sound professional judgement. Rigorous attention to detail. Data-driven decision-making and thorough documentation practices. Integrity, impartiality, and professionalism. Ability to work effectively within a multi-disciplinary regulatory environment. Ref: IT Manager Apply Now

title: Manager, Information & Communication Technology (ICT)
description: Manager, Information & Communication Technology (ICT) Jamaica Tertiary Education Commission Kingston and St. Andrew See description Fixed term contract Updated 16/02/2026 HRM Apply Now Share Share this job Facebook Twitter Report This Job Save Save Plans, coordinates and oversees the development, implementation, management and maintenance of a modern Information and Communications Technology (ICT) for the JTEC. JAMAICA TERTIARY EDUCATION COMMISSION JOB DESCRIPTION JOB TITLE: Manager, Information & Communication Technology (ICT) JOB GRADE:                          MIS/IT6 DIVISION/ SECTION:            Corporate Service Branch REPORTS TO:                          Director, Corporate Services SUPERVISES DIRECTLY:        Database Administrator ICT Technician Job Purpose Under the general direction of the Director, Corporate Services, the Manager, Information & Communication Technology (ICT), plans, co-ordinates and oversees the development, implementation, management and maintenance of a modern Information and Communications Technology (ICT) for the JTEC, based on the Business Plans/Strategies of the Department and according to the Government of Jamaica policies. Key Responsibilities Policy and Strategic Management: Participates in the development and implementation of specific Corporate/Operational Plan and Budget activities for the division; Develops and implements the Annual work plan for the Unit; Manages the operations of the Unit; Develops, implements and evaluates IT strategies/plan, policies and procedures, including those for architecture, security, disaster recovery, standards, purchasing and service provision, in accordance with Government Regulations and in keeping with the Organisation’s Objectives; Prepares and submits reports, position papers and other documents, as required; Represents the Jamaica Tertiary Education Commission at local and international meetings, conferences and other fora, as required. Technical/ Professional: Develops, implements and manages Service Level Agreements (SLAs); Reviews requests for technology and makes recommendations, as appropriate, on alternative system options; Develops computer information resources, providing for data security and control, strategic computing and disaster recovery; Facilitates the procurement of hardware/software on behalf of the Department, in accordance with the Government Procurement Procedures; Provides ICT policy direction and support services to Senior Management and Unit Heads, thereby planning and developing a variety of documents, as required; Oversees the management of ICT through the access and utilization of information systems, such as email, internet and the networking of information technology for the different Units (Unit Heads). Ensures the management team is up to date and well educated on strategic Information Technology issues; Selects, develops, implements and supports new systems and technologies; Conducts post implementation reviews to ensure that benefits are achieved; Ensures proper documentation, storage and dissemination of information pertaining to ICT systems, policies, procedures, plans and projects; Formulates and implements an ICT acquisition strategy that ensures compliance with vendor licensing requirements, as well as GOJ Procurement Guidelines; Provides technical advice to the Director, Corporate Services and other relevant personnel on ICT matters; Develops and maintains current and effective ICT training plan for the organisation; Collaborates with the relevant personnel to develop a programme for the effective communication of the Department’s policies, programmes and projects to its stakeholders; Ensures that the organisation’s website is current and accessible; Conducts primary and secondary research and analysis of ICT, and informs how these tools can be utilized in maximizing the organisation’s ICT effectiveness; Acts as resource person on selection panels service wide. Human Resource Management: Provides leadership and guidance to direct reports through effective planning, delegation, communication, training, mentoring, coaching and performance assessment; Ensures that all staff are aware of, and adhere to organisational policies and procedures; Ensures the effective utilisation of staff in the Team to maximise productivity and enhance personal development; Participates in the recruitment of staff for the Unit and recommends transfer, promotion, termination and leave in accordance with established Human Resource Policies and Procedures; Establishes and maintains a system that fosters a culture of teamwork, employee empowerment and commitment to the Unit’s and Department’s goals; Ensures the welfare and developmental needs of direct reports are clearly identified and addressed. Other: Leads/participates in internal and external cross-functional teams to represent the views and interests of the Office as required; Performs any other duties that may be assigned from time to time. Required Knowledge, Skills and Competencies Excellent leadership, management and interpersonal skills Broad-based knowledge of the future direction and use of ICT by Government and the Private Sectors Knowledge of the Government of Jamaica’s Procurement Procedures and Financial Administration and Audit Act Excellent problem-solving, strategic thinking and analytical skills Sound Human Resource/people management skills Excellent oral and written communication skills Sound personal and professional integrity Confidentiality and integrity Minimum Required Qualification and Experience Undergraduate Degree in Computer Science or Management Information Systems or equivalent qualifications and training in current technology. A Masters Degree in Information Technology or a related field would be an asset; Six (6) years’ experience in the information technology field, including three (3) years’ at the management level; Proficiency in Project Management would be an asset. SPECIAL CONDITIONS Normal office environment May be required to work beyond normal working hours, and on weekends and public holidays from time to time AUTHORITY To provide approved information to the public Ref: MICT Apply Now

title: National Irrigation Commission Ltd.
description: National Irrigation Commission Ltd. CORPORATE PROFILE The National Irrigation Commission Limited (NIC) is an agency of the Ministry of Agriculture, Fisheries and Mining. The NIC was established under the Companies Act in 1986 and is the Authority under the Irrigation Act of 1949 with the mandate to manage, operate, maintain and expand existing and future irrigation schemes and systems established by the Government of Jamaica. OUR OPERATION The NIC serves farmers with quality irrigation service to increase agricultural production and productivity. NIC’s operations cover a designated drainage area in the Upper and Lower Morass of the Black River, St. Elizabeth and sixteen (16) irrigation schemes and systems in three (3) regions: Eastern Region:           St. Catherine: Rio Cobre, St. Dorothy’s, Colbeck and Amity Hall Portland: Spring Garden St. Thomas: Yallahs, Yallahs IDB and Plantain Garden River Central:                       Clarendon: Mid Clarendon and Monymusk Manchester: New Forest/Duff House Western:                      St. Elizabeth: Hounslow, Beacon/Little Park and Holland St. James: Seven Rivers Trelawny: Braco The NIC is governed by a 15-member Board of Directors and managed by the Chief Executive Officer through five (5) Departments: Engineering and Technical Services, Finance and Corporate Planning, Projects Planning and Execution, Commercial Operations and Administration and Human Resources. title: Data Privacy Manager
description: Data Privacy Manager Planning Institute of Jamaica (PIOJ) Kingston and St. Andrew See description Permanent full-time Updated 11/02/2026 HRD Apply Now Share Share this job Facebook Twitter Report This Job Save Save Data Privacy Manager JOB PURPOSE: The Data Privacy Manager is responsible for managing privacy related activities of the PIOJ, ensuring that data privacy and security requirements are met in accordance with applicable laws, and regulations. The incumbent will be a key player in inter-organisational collaboration to ensure awareness and adoption toward best practices for data privacy. Pay Scale: Level 9 - Minimum $6,333,301 p.a. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: Manage the process of conducting Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) to identify, analyse and manage privacy risks in accordance with the existing Risk Management Framework. Monitor service developments for security and privacy compliance with Privacy by Design Develop, manage and maintain key privacy programme metrics. Coordinate with external regulatory bodies to ensure all programmes, policies, and procedures are aligned with the Data Protection Act (DPA) and all other related legislations which may impact the operations. Support the Data Protection Officer (DPO) with breach response and regulatory monitoring. Conduct periodic review of industry data privacy trends and developments, as well as applicable government regulations and recommend improvements. Coordinate with Data Privacy champions to drive compliance and privacy literacy across business operations. SKILLS AND COMPETENCIES: Extensive knowledge of the Data Protection Act, other regional and international regulations, and their applicability to the operations. Sufficient knowledge of privacy and information security technologies Ability to work independently with other leads/liaisons to discuss and resolve privacy issues. Strong working knowledge of assessing data protection risks and advising on organisational controls. Knowledge of security industry regulations/standards (NIST Privacy Framework) and compliance frameworks (ISO 27701, etc.) Excellent written and verbal communication skills QUALIFICATION AND EXPERIENCE: Bachelor’s Degree in Public Policy and Management, Organisational Psychology, Law or related field Minimum 5-7 years’ experience in the related field Privacy certifications or Certified Information Privacy Manager (CIPM) Experience in data protection, compliance, governance or related field Experience with addressing privacy standards issues and liaising with standard bodies Experience in a legal, audit, or risk management role would be an asset Strong understanding of and adherence to ethical codes and practices Proficiency with Microsoft suite of products with excellent skills in Excel Applications should be submitted no later than February 25, 2026 to: Senior Director, Human Resources Planning Institute of Jamaica 16 Oxford Road Kingston 5 We thank you for your expressions of interest, however, only shortlisted applicants will be contacted. Ref: Data Privacy Manager Apply Now

title: Service Desk Coordinator
description: Service Desk Coordinator BLEWSTREAM ECOSYSTEM Port-of-Spain / San Fernando / Trincity Not disclosed Permanent full-time Updated 09/02/2026 Apply Now Share Share this job Facebook Twitter Report This Job Save Save We are looking for a Service Desk Coordinator to join our IT team. This role is responsible for escalating, and resolving complex and high impact incidents, across many platforms, while ensuring clear and consistent communication with stakeholders. Position Title: Service Desk Coordinator Location: 26 Bay Road, St. James, Port of Spain, 16 Orange Grove Rd, Tacarigua (Trintoplan Compound), 14- 16 Turney Street, San Fernando Employment Type: Full-Time, Hybrid Role Overview: The Service Desk Coordinator is the technical and operational owner of service delivery across the Service Desk. This role is responsible for coordinating, escalating, and resolving complex and high impact incidents, particularly across Microsoft 365, Intune, Sophos, identity, access, and endpoint management platforms, while ensuring clear, timely, and consistent communication with stakeholders. The role also provides day-to-day technical and operational guidance to junior service desk staff, supporting quality, consistency, and adherence to SLAs. Combining hands-on technical capability, service coordination, and stakeholder communication, the Service Desk Coordinator acts as the primary escalation point between the Service Desk, internal teams, external vendors, and management. Key Responsibilities: 1. Service Delivery, Incident & Escalation Management Act as the primary escalation point for complex, high-impact, or unresolved incidents. Provide 2nd and 3rd level support where required, including hands-on technical resolution. Partner with business stakeholders and end users to: understand incident impact, prioritise appropriately, and ensure quality of service and customer satisfaction Exercise sound judgment where impact, value, or risk is not immediately clear. 2. Technical Resolution & Platform Administration Perform hands-on troubleshooting and resolution for complex service desk issues that exceed 1st-line capability. Validate client and internal configurations against documented standards. Identify recurring issues and drive root-cause remediation, not just ticket closure. Support employee onboarding and access provisioning workflows where escalation or exception handling is required. 3. SLA, Queue & Priority Management Own the service desk queue, ensuring tickets are correctly logged, categorised, and prioritised, and SLAs and OLAs are actively monitored and enforced Reassign or escalate tickets as required to meet service targets. Identify SLA risks early and take corrective action. 4. Communication & Stakeholder Management Provide clear, timely updates to the organisation on major incidents, high-impact escalations and service disruptions and resolutions Liaise with client IT teams for access provisioning, application issues, and configuration dependencies. Escalate risks, recurring issues, and service gaps to the IT Manager with evidence-based recommendations. 5. Governance, Documentation & Continuous Improvement Maintain and improve knowledge base articles, escalation procedures and client-specific configuration documentation Contribute to incident reviews, problem management and service improvement initiatives Support audits, compliance requirements, and service reporting. Required Experience & Skills Experience (Essential) 3–5 years’ experience in a service desk, IT support, or service delivery role Demonstrated experience handling escalations and complex incidents Experience working with vendors and third parties Demonstrated experience overseeing day-to-day work of service desk or IT support staff, including workload coordination, escalation support, and performance feedback. Technical Skills (Essential) Strong administration and troubleshooting experience with Microsoft 365 (Entra ID, Exchange, Teams, SharePoint), Intune / endpoint management as well as endpoint security platforms (Sophos preferred) Solid understanding of incident, escalation, and service delivery practices Education Requirements (Required) Bachelor’s Degree in Information Technology, Computer Science or Information Systems OR equivalent professional experience Preferred / Advantageous ITIL Foundation certification Microsoft certifications (e.g. M365, Azure, Endpoint Administration) Soft Skills Excellent written and verbal communication Strong ownership and accountability Strong customer service orientation Ability to translate technical issues into business-focused updates Confident decision-making under pressure Ability to prioritize and/or make judgement calls when impact/value/risk are less clear Why Join Us? Be part of a collaborative IT team focused on operational excellence. Competitive compensation, benefits, and opportunities for professional development. Hybrid work environment promoting flexibility and work-life balance. Ref: BH#458 Apply Now

title: Information Technology Specialist (Guyana)
description: Information Technology Specialist (Guyana) S.M. Jaleel & Company Limited Guyana / Georgetown Not disclosed Permanent full-time Updated 12/02/2026 HR Department Apply Now Share Share this job Facebook Twitter Report This Job Save Save The Information Technology Specialist is responsible for overseeing IT operations while also providing first-line technical support to onsite and remote employees in Guyana. IT Specialist (Guyana) Location: Georgetown Position Overview The Information Technology Specialist is responsible for overseeing IT operations while also providing first-line technical support to onsite and remote employees in Guyana. This role ensures secure, reliable, and efficient technology services across the organization, including oversight of IT infrastructure, systems administration, ERP support, team coordination, and compliance with IT standards and policies. Key Responsibilities IT Management & Leadership Lead day-to-day IT operations for the Guyana site, ensuring alignment with organizational goals and global IT standards. Develop, implement, and enforce IT policies, procedures, and best practices. Oversee IT projects, including system upgrades, infrastructure improvements, and technology rollouts. Manage IT vendors, service providers, and procurement of IT equipment and software. Supervise IT staff where applicable and coordinate support activities across teams. Conduct ongoing risk assessments to improve system security, reliability, and performance. ERP Administration & Support Provide functional and technical support for the organization’s ERP system. Coordinate ERP upgrades, module enhancements, and integration initiatives. Troubleshoot ERP user issues and collaborate with business units to optimize workflows. Maintain user roles, permissions, and access governance within the ERP platform. Generate ERP-related reports, audits, and data analysis as required. Technical Support & Operations Configure and maintain end-user devices, including laptops, mobile phones, printers, and associated profiles. Troubleshoot and resolve hardware, software, network, and authentication issues. Support onboarding and offboarding through accurate provisioning and deactivation of accounts. Provide administration support for Quickbooks. Maintain time and attendance Facial Recognition devices. Support GPS Tracking software – Platform Admin, vendor relations and coordination Provide CCTV maintenance, configuration and support. Maintain precise documentation of incidents, resolutions, and system changes. Perform routine preventive maintenance and ensure system uptime. Provide technical support to the Delivery and Sales teams as required. Asset & Security Management Oversee IT asset management, including inventory control, allocation, and lifecycle tracking. Ensure adherence to cybersecurity standards, data protection practices, and regulatory requirements. Implement and monitor backup strategies, disaster recovery procedures, and business continuity controls. Ethics & Confidentiality Uphold organizational standards and maintain strict confidentiality at all times. Education, Qualifications and knowledge requirements: Bachelor’s degree in information technology, Computer Science, or a related field. Professional certifications (CompTIA A+, Network+, ITIL, Microsoft, Cisco) are an asset. Minimum of five (5) years of experience in IT operations, technical support, or IT management. Hands-on experience with ERP systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle, Microsoft Dynamics, or equivalent) is required. Experience managing IT Projects, Strong knowledge of IT operational processes, resource management, and performance monitoring. Demonstrated experience troubleshooting to resolve complex issues. Continuously identify opportunities for optimization and automation. Excellent critical thinking, problem-solving, decision-making, and organizational abilities. Strong communication, interpersonal and collaboration skills. High level of integrity, adaptability, and results orientation. Applications are open to Guyanese nationals only. Ref: Information Technology Specialist (Guyana) Apply Now
